questions.question: What are the major physical features of India?
questions.answer: The major physical features of India are the Himalayas, Northern Plains, Peninsular Plateau, Indian Desert, Coastal Plains, and Islands.

questions.question: Why does India have great physical diversity?
questions.answer: India has great physical diversity because it contains mountains, plains, plateaus, deserts, coastal areas, and islands.

questions.question: Why are the Himalayas called young fold mountains?
questions.answer: The Himalayas are called young fold mountains because they were formed by the folding of the earth's crust due to tectonic movements.

questions.question: Name the three ranges of the Himalayas.
questions.answer: The three ranges of the Himalayas are Himadri, Himachal, and Shiwaliks.

questions.question: What is the Himadri?
questions.answer: The Himadri is the northernmost and highest range of the Himalayas with an average height of about 6000 metres.

questions.question: What is the Himachal range?
questions.answer: The Himachal range is the middle Himalayan range known for valleys and hill stations.

questions.question: What are the Shiwaliks?
questions.answer: The Shiwaliks are the outermost range of the Himalayas made of unconsolidated sediments.

questions.question: How were the Northern Plains formed?
questions.answer: The Northern Plains were formed by the deposition of alluvial soil brought by the Indus, Ganga, and Brahmaputra rivers.

questions.question: Why are the Northern Plains fertile?
questions.answer: They are fertile because rivers deposit rich alluvial soil every year.

questions.question: What is Bhabar?
questions.answer: Bhabar is a narrow belt of pebbles deposited by rivers along the foothills of the Himalayas.

questions.question: What is Terai?
questions.answer: Terai is a marshy and swampy region south of the Bhabar belt.

questions.question: What is Bhangar?
questions.answer: Bhangar is the older alluvial soil found in the Northern Plains.

questions.question: What is Khadar?
questions.answer: Khadar is the newer alluvial soil deposited by rivers every year.

questions.question: What is the Peninsular Plateau?
questions.answer: The Peninsular Plateau is an ancient tableland composed of igneous and metamorphic rocks.

questions.question: Name the two divisions of the Peninsular Plateau.
questions.answer: Central Highlands and Deccan Plateau.

questions.question: What are the Western Ghats?
questions.answer: The Western Ghats are a mountain range along the western edge of the Peninsular Plateau.

questions.question: What are the Eastern Ghats?
questions.answer: The Eastern Ghats are a discontinuous mountain range along the eastern edge of the Peninsular Plateau.

questions.question: What is the Indian Desert?
questions.answer: The Indian Desert is a dry region with sand dunes and very low rainfall located in western India.

questions.question: Which is the main river of the Indian Desert?
questions.answer: The Luni River.

questions.question: What are coastal plains?
questions.answer: Coastal plains are narrow strips of land along the sea coast.

questions.question: Name the two coastal plains of India.
questions.answer: Western Coastal Plains and Eastern Coastal Plains.

questions.question: Which is the largest saltwater lake in India?
questions.answer: Chilika Lake.

questions.question: Name the two island groups of India.
questions.answer: Lakshadweep Islands and Andaman & Nicobar Islands.

questions.question: What type of islands are Lakshadweep?
questions.answer: Lakshadweep are coral islands.

questions.question: Why are physical features important for India?
questions.answer: They influence climate, agriculture, natural resources, and human settlements.
